_field_guide.bible_id: Unique ID, format: loc_{number}, e.g. loc_001
_field_guide.location_name: Human-readable name, e.g. 'Jesse's Apartment — Living Room'
_field_guide.passport.time_of_day: Array of: DAY, NIGHT, DAWN, DUSK, CONTINUOUS
_field_guide.passport.era: Specific period, e.g. '2004 Albuquerque' or '1962 Brooklyn'
_field_guide.passport.recurring: true if location appears in 3+ scenes
_field_guide.passport.scenes: Array of scene IDs where this location appears
_field_guide.passport.cluster_id: ID of location cluster if part of a group (e.g. 'Jesse's apartment' cluster includes living room, bedroom, bathroom)
_field_guide.space_description: 400-600 words. Dense, specific. Every sentence = visual information for generation. Cover: dimensions, layout, materials, condition, wear patterns, color of walls/floor/ceiling
_field_guide.atmosphere: 100-200 words. How the space FEELS — temperature, smells, sounds, emotional quality. Sensory, not abstract
_field_guide.light_base_state.primary_source: Main light: 'window', 'overhead fluorescent', 'table lamp', 'neon sign', etc.
_field_guide.light_base_state.direction: Cardinal: N, S, E, W, NE, NW, SE, SW, OVERHEAD, MULTIPLE
_field_guide.light_base_state.practical_sources: Array of in-scene light sources: ['desk lamp with green shade', 'TV glow', 'window — east facing']
_field_guide.key_details: Array of 5-8 strings. SPECIFIC objects: 'rotary phone with frayed cord' not 'a phone'. These details make the space unique and recognizable across shots
_field_guide.negative_list: Array of strings. Things that must NEVER appear. Added mechanically to negative_prompt of every generation. e.g. ['modern appliances', 'LED lights', 'smartphones', 'flat screen TV']
